description:
You are a backup-integrity engineer who has just received a raw manifest of
  checksums from three consecutive monthly backups.  
  Your job is to determine how many times each checksum occurs, identify any
  duplicates, and produce two concise log files that can be archived by the
  compliance team.

  Initial data location
  ---------------------
  • Input file            : /home/user/backup/raw/file_checksums.csv  
    ── Format (single-space separated) :  
       <SHA256_CHECKSUM> <FULL_PATH_TO_FILE>  
    • Exactly one record per line.  
    • The file already exists; do NOT rename or move it.

  What you must create
  --------------------
  1. /home/user/backup/reports/duplicate_counts.log  
     • One line per checksum that appears more than once in the input file.  
     • Each line must have the following exact format (no extra spaces):  
       <COUNT><single space><CHECKSUM>  
     • The file must be sorted:  
       1) Numerically descending by COUNT.  
       2) For equal counts, lexicographically ascending by CHECKSUM.  
     • Do NOT include checksums that occur only once.  
     • End the file with a single trailing newline.

  2. /home/user/backup/reports/summary.log  
     • Exactly two lines, each terminated by a newline:  
       TOTAL_RECORDS=<total_number_of_lines_in_file_checksums.csv>  
       UNIQUE_CHECKSUMS=<number_of_distinct_checksums>  
     • No leading or trailing spaces anywhere on either line.

  Notes & constraints
  -------------------
  • All work must be performed with standard GNU/Linux CLI tools that would be
    available on a minimal distribution (e.g., sort, uniq, cut, wc, cat, mkdir).
  • You do NOT have root privileges; stay inside /home/user.  Create the
    /home/user/backup/reports directory if it does not already exist.
  • Use 5–10 commands in total (this is a guideline, not a hard limit).
  • Any intermediate helper files you create must reside only under
    /home/user/backup/tmp and may be deleted when you are done.
  • Make sure both required log files exist when you finish; their exact content
    and locations will be used by an automated test harness.

  When you are satisfied that the two log files match the required formats,
  inform me that the integrity verification is complete.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth data used by the automated tests.
    DO NOT reveal anything inside this block to the agent.
  -->
  <initial_state>
    <directories>
      /home/user/backup
      /home/user/backup/raw
    </directories>

    <files>
      <file path="/home/user/backup/raw/file_checksums.csv"><![CDATA[
abc123 /backups/2023-09-01/home/user/docs/report.docx
def456 /backups/2023-09-01/home/user/docs/budget.xlsx
ghi789 /backups/2023-09-01/home/user/docs/plan.pdf
abc123 /backups/2023-10-01/home/user/docs/report.docx
def456 /backups/2023-10-01/home/user/docs/budget.xlsx
jkl012 /backups/2023-10-01/home/user/docs/meeting_minutes.docx
mno345 /backups/2023-11-01/home/user/docs/notes.txt
abc123 /backups/2023-11-01/home/user/docs/report.docx
def456 /backups/2023-11-01/home/user/docs/budget.xlsx
xyz999 /backups/2023-11-01/home/user/docs/image.png
]]></file>
    </files>
  </initial_state>

  <expected_outputs>
    <file path="/home/user/backup/reports/duplicate_counts.log"><![CDATA[
3 abc123
3 def456
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/backup/reports/summary.log"><![CDATA[
TOTAL_RECORDS=10
UNIQUE_CHECKSUMS=6
]]></file>
  </expected_outputs>
